session_summary: An adversarial verification session: Marcus commissioned an independent audit of the AI-assisted (A)+(C) MAR information-convention resolution (todo/018, commit d37183b), explicitly forbidding trust in the commit message or prior summaries and demanding per-claim refutation from literature, code, and reproductions. The human's role was to design and enforce the verification protocol (adversarial stance, blind cross-model independence checks, citation/render gates) and then to request the fixes be packaged as a portable correction prompt. The audit returned PASS with two material WARNs (a line-906 contradiction and an overstated GPT-5.5 independence claim).

coded_turns.quote:
independently verify or refute each claim from the literature, the code, and reproductions. Your job is to find
    errors, not to confirm.
coded_turns.note: Commissions an adversarial audit forbidding trust in prior summaries; pre-specifies independence, mapping, and weak-point attacks.

coded_turns.quote: Yes but remember, you are using the derivation-audit skill
coded_turns.note: Redirects the AI to run the audit through the prescribed derivation-audit skill rather than an ad hoc workflow.

ai_side.evidence: so "independently returns the same closed form" and "spontaneously the K&M recommendation" overstate it. GPT produced no number; the agreement is structural

ai_side.evidence: with the two material WARNs (line-906 contradiction; overstated GPT-5.5 independence) as the actionable items.
ai_side.note: AI surfaces two material defects in committed text despite the overall PASS verdict.

ai_side.note: AI explicitly raises a disagreement with the prior audit for the human to adjudicate.
